title: salix × obtusata
intro: Salix × obtusata, also known as the weeping willow hybrid, is a cross between the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the white willow (Salix alba), resulting in a fast-growing, deciduous shrub or small tree. The bark and leaves of the Salix × obtusata plant have been used in traditional herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat infections. The plant's medicinal properties are due in part to its high salicylic acid content, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been shown to provide a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, headaches, and skin conditions.
